patternCompliance.notes: All 4 new workflow functions return {enabled: false, error: ...} on failure
No external dependencies in any new module
All 4 new commands follow graph-* naming convention

principleNotes.notes: Minimal implementations — BFS with deque, AST-based signature parsing, no over-engineering
Changes confined to planned file scope, no drive-by edits
All 4 plans used TDD contract with failing/passing verify cycles

evidence: Plan 01: test file split (1026-line → 4 modules + conftest) + test coverage mapping — 56 tests pass, all modules under 800 lines
Plan 02: visualization with Mermaid + DOT output — 32 tests pass including E2E integration
Plan 03: API contract break detection + auto_populate suggest_scope — 25 tests pass
Plan 04: BFS proximity ranking for context optimization — 25 tests pass
All 4 CLI commands verified (graph-test-coverage, graph-viz, graph-contract-check, graph-prioritize)
No scope violations — all changes within declared plan files[]
stageReviews.notes: All 4 plans fully implemented matching CONTEXT.json decisions. 6 decisions fulfilled, 3 constraints honored (stdlib-only, graph-* naming, graceful degradation).

findings.description: contracts.py has a dead elif branch in first ast.walk pass (line 55-59) — functional but could be cleaner

evidence: Correctness: 2/2 — 138 tests pass, BFS algorithms correct, edge cases handled
Security: 2/2 — parameterized SQL throughout, no injection vectors, stdlib-only
Contract Compliance: 2/2 — all 4 plan contracts fulfilled, artifacts present
Test Coverage: 2/2 — comprehensive suite with dedicated tests per feature
Code Quality: 2/2 — clean structure, consistent patterns, proper type annotations
Performance: 2/2 — efficient BFS with deque + visited, batch SQL queries
Documentation: 1/2 — good docstrings, minor gap: no overview tying 4 capabilities together
stageReviews.notes: Score: 13/14. All critical axes (Correctness, Security, Contract Compliance) at maximum. Single minor documentation gap is non-blocking.
